questions.question_text: Does Tennessee require the use of state-mandated contract forms for real estate transactions?
questions.correct_answer: No, Tennessee does not mandate specific forms
distractors: Yes, TREC-approved forms are mandatory
Yes, all contracts must use TAR forms
Only for properties over $250,000
questions.explanation: Unlike Texas, Tennessee does not require state-mandated contract forms. The Tennessee Association of Realtors (TAR) publishes standard forms, but their use is voluntary.

questions.question_text: What standard contract forms are most commonly used in Tennessee real estate transactions?
distractors: TREC-mandated standard forms
PSI-approved contract templates
Locally drafted county forms
questions.explanation: TAR publishes standard forms that are widely used in Tennessee practice. While not mandatory, they are the industry standard and provide comprehensive contract terms.

questions.question_text: An affiliate broker drafts a custom legal clause for a purchase agreement at the buyer's request. Is this permitted?
distractors: Yes, if the buyer requests it in writing
Yes, for clauses under $10,000 in value
Only with the principal broker's approval
questions.explanation: Agents may fill in blanks on standard forms but cannot draft custom legal provisions. Drafting legal clauses constitutes the unauthorized practice of law.

questions.question_text: Under the Statute of Frauds, Tennessee real estate contracts must be:
distractors: Notarized by a public notary
Filed with the Register of Deeds
Approved by TREC before signing
questions.explanation: The Statute of Frauds requires contracts for the sale of real property to be in writing to be enforceable. Oral agreements for real estate are generally not enforceable.

questions.question_text: A buyer and seller verbally agree to a home sale over the phone. Is this contract enforceable in Tennessee?
questions.correct_answer: No, real estate contracts must be in writing
distractors: Yes, if both parties agree verbally
Yes, if confirmed by email within 48 hours
Only for properties under $100,000
questions.explanation: Under the Statute of Frauds, verbal agreements for the sale of real property are not enforceable in Tennessee. The contract must be in writing and signed by the parties.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT one of the essential elements of a valid real estate contract?
distractors: Offer and acceptance
Consideration
Legal capacity of the parties
questions.explanation: While earnest money is customary in Tennessee, it is not legally required to form a valid contract. The essential elements are offer and acceptance, consideration, capacity, and lawful purpose.

questions.question_text: A buyer submits an offer contingent on obtaining financing. If financing is denied, what happens?
questions.correct_answer: The buyer can terminate without breach
distractors: The seller can sue for specific performance
The buyer forfeits the earnest money
The contract automatically extends 30 days
questions.explanation: A financing contingency protects the buyer. If financing is denied, the buyer can terminate the contract without being in breach, typically receiving their earnest money back.

questions.question_text: Is earnest money required to form a valid real estate contract in Tennessee?
questions.correct_answer: No, it is customary but not legally required
distractors: Yes, at least 1% of the purchase price
Yes, at least $1,000 on all transactions
Yes, unless both parties waive it
questions.explanation: Earnest money is customary in Tennessee practice but is not legally required to form a valid contract. A contract can exist without an earnest money deposit.

questions.question_text: A contract includes a 'time is of the essence' clause. What does this mean for the parties?
questions.correct_answer: Deadlines in the contract must be strictly met
distractors: The closing must occur within 30 days
Either party may cancel at any time
Extensions are automatically granted if needed
questions.explanation: A 'time is of the essence' clause means that deadlines are strict — missing a deadline could constitute a breach of contract. This is commonly included in Tennessee real estate contracts.

questions.question_text: A purchase agreement includes inspection, financing, and appraisal contingencies. If the home appraises below the purchase price, what can the buyer do under the appraisal contingency?
questions.correct_answer: Negotiate the price or terminate the contract
distractors: Only proceed at the original price
Automatically receive the difference in cash
File a complaint with TREC about the seller
questions.explanation: An appraisal contingency allows the buyer to negotiate a lower price or terminate the contract if the appraisal comes in below the purchase price, protecting them from overpaying.

questions.question_text: A seller receives three offers simultaneously. What must the listing agent do?
questions.correct_answer: Present all three offers promptly to the seller
distractors: Present only the highest offer first
Present them in the order received
Hold them until all offers are complete
questions.explanation: All offers must be presented to the seller promptly. The agent cannot withhold, delay, or prioritize any offer based on the agent's judgment — the seller decides.

questions.question_text: A seller receives an offer $50,000 below asking price. The listing agent thinks it is unreasonable. Must it still be presented?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, all offers must be presented regardless
distractors: No, clearly lowball offers can be declined
Only if the buyer's agent insists
Only if the seller specifically requested all offers
questions.explanation: The seller has the right to see and respond to every offer. A licensee cannot withhold or delay presentation of any offer based on their opinion of its reasonableness.

questions.question_text: After receiving multiple offers, what rights does the seller have?
questions.correct_answer: Accept, reject, or counter any offer
distractors: Must accept the highest offer received
Must counter all offers equally
Must accept the first offer received
questions.explanation: The seller has the right to accept, reject, or counter any offer. There is no obligation to accept the highest offer or to treat all offers the same way.

questions.question_text: A listing agent receives a verbal offer from a buyer's agent. Must this be presented to the seller?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, even verbal offers should be communicated
distractors: No, only written offers must be presented
Only if the buyer follows up in writing
Only if the amount is within 10% of asking
questions.explanation: While written offers are preferred, agents should communicate all offers to the seller, including verbal ones. The agent's duty is to keep the seller informed of all buyer interest.

questions.question_text: An agent delays presenting an offer hoping their own buyer will submit a higher one. What violation has occurred?
distractors: Smart business strategy for the seller
Acceptable if the higher offer materializes
Only a violation if the seller finds out
questions.explanation: Withholding or delaying offers is prohibited. Additionally, delaying to benefit the agent's own buyer constitutes self-dealing, a serious violation of fiduciary duties and TREC rules.
